page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 48 dedicated physical cores based on the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture (AWS Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache, with no hyperthreading. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are included.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8gb.12xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, reaching 407.39 GB/sec for large block reads and 196.56 GB/sec for large block writes. Multi-threaded operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a top-tier multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 520.64 GB/sec and a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 81853.63. The instance also excels in software compilation, scoring 70645.0 in Geekbench clang compilation, and shows strong capabilities in database operations and static web serving throughput. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations fall within the average range.

Given its ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation, the instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded, compute-heavy workloads. The high memory bandwidth and 100 Gbps network baseline make it highly suitable for high-throughput web serving, software compilation pipelines, database operations, and multi-threaded data decompression. However, the lack of local storage and GPU acceleration limits its suitability for heavy local disk-bound databases or GPU-dependent deep learning training.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, 96.0 GB of system memory, and a 100 Gbps baseline network capacity, operating on the Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the c8gb.12xlarge delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, reaching 407.39 GB/sec for large block reads, alongside strong multi-core CPU performance and high software compilation speeds. It is highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, showing excellent results in database operations and static web serving. This instance is best suited for high-throughput web serving, distributed data processing, software compilation, and multi-threaded decompression tasks where dedicated ARM64 compute resources are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 96 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.12xlarge features 48 dedicated ARM64 cores, 96 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized platform featuring 48 dedicated cores and high-speed memory bandwidth for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM64 physical cores utilizing AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors.
96.0 GB of system memory paired with a 100 Gbps baseline network interface.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression performance.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
